title: The Children in the Marketplace
subtitle: Christ rebukes the generation that would neither mourn with John nor rejoice with him

stillness: Be still, and let the marketplace empty.
Two songs are sung over you: one of mourning, one of joy.
gospel: This generation is like children sitting in the marketplace,
who cry out to their companions and say:
We have piped to you, and you have not danced;
we have mourned, and you have not wept.
For John came neither eating nor drinking,
and they say: He has a demon.
The Son of Man came eating and drinking,
and they say: Behold a man who is a glutton,
a wine-drinker, a friend of publicans and sinners.
behold: Behold the children of the Hebrews, gathered in the middle of the city.
They are divided into two equal parts.
Those on one side sing a song of gladness;
those on the opposite side sing a song of mourning,
mocking the sudden change of this present life.
And afterward they reproach one another:
the joyful ask why the others would not rejoice with them;
the mourners ask why the others would not grieve with them.
See now the prophets seated in the marketplace, which is the Church,
where the law and its rulings are spoken aloud.
They sing, promising the everlasting joys, and call you to exult;
they mourn, threatening the torments, and call you to grieve for sin.
Behold John, fasting and abstaining, inviting you to weep for your sins;
behold the Lord, eating and drinking among men, inviting you to rejoice for grace.
He lived the common life among men,
so that sinners living the common life might come to Him familiarly.
consider: John piped the lament of penance; Christ piped the gladness of mercy.
But the Jewish generation does not weep with John weeping,
They saw the austerity of John, and were not moved to penance.
They saw the sweetness of Christ, and were not moved to pity.
They despised both preachings, and held them both as nothing.
John came in great abstinence, and they said: He has a demon.
As if to say: like the demons he neither eats nor drinks; he is mad.
The Lord came gentle and mild, and they said: Behold a glutton, a wine-drinker.
A friend of publicans and sinners, by evil company.
So the song was sung and they did not dance;
the lament was raised and they did not weep.
And what of you, wretched sinner?
Have you risen to the praise of God when the song was piped?
Have you wept and done penance when the lament was raised?
Those who sow in tears shall reap in exultation.
prayer: who by word and example have called us
to grieve for our sins and to rejoice for spiritual goods:
give me the grace of tears,
and water the dry ground of my heart with the dew of heaven,
that I may weep for myself every day of my whole life;
so that my tears may become my bread day and night,
and my mind, forgetful of vanity and misery, may burn in your love;
so that, dancing toward good works and rising to the praises of God,
I may now exult in hope,
and at the last, praising you forever, may rejoice with you without end.

latinJewel.latin: Sed generatio Judaica non plorat cum Joanne plorante, neque gaudet cum Christo gaudente.
latinJewel.english: But the Jewish generation does not weep with John weeping, nor rejoice with Christ rejoicing.
